Off Calendar Oct-05-2023 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Request For Dismissal Of Class Action Pursuant To California Rules Of Court, Rule 3.770
Off Calendar Sep-18-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFFS JACKIE WILLIAMS AND DAWN DURBIN'S Motion For Request For Dismissal Of Class Action Pursuant To California Rules Of Court, Rule 3.770.

Plaintiffs' unopposed motion "for dismissal of class action pursuant to" CRC 3.770 is granted.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/RBU)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-13-2023 Continued To Aug-30-23 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-05-2022 Continued To Apr-26-23 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Motion And Notice Of Hearing(W/First Appearance Fee) (Motions)
Off Calendar Sep-06-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. STEVEN B. KATZ, Esq. of Constangy, Brooks, Smith & Prophete LLP (Tel: 310-909-7775) for defendant First American Title Insurance Company;
No other appearances made in this matter.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT FIRST AMERICAN TITLE INSURANCE COMPANY's Motion to Compel Arbitration.

Matter discussed. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant's motion to compel arbitration is granted in part. The court compels arbitration of plaintiffs' individual PAGA claims and stays the remainder of this case, which implicates the representative PAGA claims. Plaintiffs apparently concede that their individual claims are arbitrable. The court declines to dismiss plaintiffs' representative claims because they still have standing pursuant to Kim v. Reins Int'l California, Inc. (2020) 9 Cal.5th 73, 86. Plaintiffs are allegedly "aggrieved employee[s]" per Labor Code sec. 2699(a), (c). Plaintiffs do not cease to be aggrieved employees merely because their individual claims are compelled to arbitration. In Kim, the court held that an employee who settles and dismisses his individual claim still has standing to maintain a representative action. (See Kim, 9 Cal.5th at 80 ["Settlement of individual claims does not strip an aggrieved employee of standing, as the state's authorized representative, to pursue PAGA remedies."].) Similarly, employees that are compelled to arbitrate their individual claims are not stripped of standing to maintain a representative action.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)
